title: jasione crispa subsp. tomentosa
intro: Jasione crispa subsp. tomentosa, also known as woolly bellflower, is a plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, with a long history of use in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Jasione crispa subsp. tomentosa include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wound healing, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, have been traditionally used to support immune function and overall well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
